 {"id":"bd-1ho","title":"Rich Core Infrastructure (rich_rust port)","description":"# Rich Core Infrastructure (reference extraction, feature-gated)

## Purpose
This epic tracks optional infrastructure ideas inspired by `rich_rust`.

The plan keeps rich_rust’s best abstractions (Segments / measurement protocol / markup parser), but de-scopes “port everything” into feature-gated extras.

## Reference Source
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/`

## Plan Alignment
From the plan (Chapter 1.4 / 1.5 / Phase 7):
- Segments + measurement protocol are valuable
- export-friendly representations are valuable
- “widget zoo / markdown / syntax / forms” are extras

We treat this epic as Phase 7 **extras infrastructure** unless/until a specific piece is required by the agent harness.

## Tracked Issues (this epic’s children)
- `bd-1ho.2` Console abstraction (optional ergonomic output helpers)
- `bd-1ho.5` Live display system (optional “live updating” view patterns)
- `bd-1ho.6` Filesize formatter (small utility; optional)
- `bd-1ho.7` Logging integration (optional formatting/bridge on top of `tracing`)
- `bd-1ho.8` Extra test suite coverage for the above

Related (rich-derived but implemented elsewhere in the plan tree):
- `bd-1ho.1` Segment system (Cow<str>)
- `bd-1ho.3` TextMeasurement protocol

## Non-Goals
- Locking the kernel behind a large “rich port”
- Introducing new, parallel APIs that duplicate ftui core

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each child issue clearly states how it integrates with ftui (crate/module + feature flag).
- [ ] Optional infrastructure cannot violate one-writer rule or inline-mode invariants.
- [ ] Tests cover behavior and failure modes; logs are actionable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:02:34.577032474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:51:42.109078852Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:51:42.108881641Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":309,"issue_id":"bd-1ho","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"},{"id":310,"issue_id":"bd-1ho","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"All 8 subtasks closed (bd-1ho.1 through bd-1ho.8).  {"id":"bd-1ho.1","title":"Implement Segment system (Cow<str> styled text units)","description":"# Implement Segment system (Cow<str> styled text units)

## Background
Plan Appendix D.1 describes a Segment system inspired by rich_rust.

A Segment is the atomic unit of styled text that can be:
- cheaply borrowed (`Cow<str>`) for string literals / static content
- split at **cell positions** (not byte positions) for correct wrapping

Segments are a bridge:
- higher-level text/layout systems produce Segments
- widgets ultimately paint to `Frame`/`Buffer` using measured/split segments

## Core types
### Segment
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct Segment<'a> {
    pub text: Cow<'a, str>,
    pub style: Option<Style>,
    pub control: Option<SmallVec<[ControlCode; 2]>>, // optional; stack-alloc common case
}
```

### Control codes (optional)
Segments may carry control codes for non-textual actions (rare; typically not needed in the kernel).
If we keep them, they must be constrained and documented.

## Key APIs
- `cell_length()` (display width)
- `split_at_cell(cell_pos)` (critical for wrapping with wide chars/ZWJ)
- helpers to split/join lines of segments

## Design constraints
- Splitting MUST respect Unicode scalar boundaries; ideally grapheme boundaries.
- Control segments must not consume width.
- ASCII width fast-path is a later optimization (see bd-v6y) and must not change correctness.

## Tests
- split_at_cell correctness for:
  - ASCII
  - CJK wide chars
  - emoji/ZWJ sequences
- cell_length matches `unicode_width` for the same input
- line splitting preserves styles and does not drop content

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Segment type exists with `Cow<str>` storage.
- [ ] `cell_length` and `split_at_cell` APIs exist and are tested.
- [ ] Segment line-splitting/joining helpers exist.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] Create segment with owned String
- [ ] Create segment with borrowed &str (Cow optimization)
- [ ] Create control segments (newline, home, bell)
- [ ] Calculate cell length accounting for:
  - ASCII characters (1 cell each)
  - Wide characters (2 cells each)
  - Zero-width characters (0 cells)
  - Control segments (0 cells)
- [ ] Split segment at grapheme boundary
- [ ] Strip styles preserving text
- [ ] Join multiple segments
- [ ] Split segments on newlines into Lines
- [ ] Join Lines back into segments

### Zero-Copy Requirements
- [ ] String literals never allocate
- [ ] Short strings use Cow::Borrowed when possible
- [ ] Split returns borrowed segments when feasible
- [ ] Concatenation minimizes copies

### Performance Requirements
- [ ] Cell length calculation: < 50ns for typical segment
- [ ] Split operation: O(1) for ASCII content
- [ ] Join operation: O(n) where n = total chars

### Logging Requirements
- [ ] tracing::trace! for segment creation (debug builds)
- [ ] tracing::warn! for unexpectedly long segments (> 10KB)

### Test Coverage
- [ ] Unit tests for all segment operations
- [ ] Property test: split-join is identity
- [ ] Property test: cell length >= 0
- [ ] Corpus test: Unicode edge cases (ZWJ, RTL, combining)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:02:46.925829242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T17:50:25.418469745Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T17:50:25.418450880Z","close_reason":"Implemented Segment system with Cow<str> storage, cell_length(), split_at_cell(), SegmentLine, SegmentLines, and line splitting utilities - 40 tests passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.1","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.1","depends_on_id":"bd-10x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ho.2","title":"Implement Console abstraction","description":"# Implement Console abstraction (Segment-first, one-writer safe)

## Objective
Provide an optional, ergonomic “console output” helper inspired by rich-style APIs, but adapted to ftui’s constraints:
- **Segment-first** (structured text + style)
- **one-writer safe** (no ad-hoc terminal writes)

This is primarily useful for:
- formatting tool output / logs into wrapped lines
- producing captured output for tests
- generating exportable text streams

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/src/console.rs`

## Scope
- A `Console` type that:
  - accepts `Segment` streams (and/or `Renderable` → `Segment`)
  - performs width-aware wrapping
  - maintains an internal style stack for nested styling
  - can operate in “record/capture” mode for tests

## Output Policy (Critical)
- The Console must not bypass ftui’s output routing.
- Any “print” API must target:
  - an in-memory buffer, OR
  - a `LogSink`/writer abstraction that is already sanitized and one-writer compliant.

## Dependencies / Integration
- Depends on Segment system and markup parsing.
- Should integrate with ftui-style and ftui-text measurement.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for wrapping behavior (including wide/combining chars via existing width utilities).
- Tests for style stack push/pop correctness.
- Capture-mode tests that assert deterministic output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `Console` exists with width-aware wrapping and a style stack.
- [ ] Capture/record mode exists and is used in tests.
- [ ] No API writes directly to stdout/stderr; output routing is explicit.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] Width detection from terminal or fallback to 80
- [ ] Explicit width override
- [ ] Print plain strings with automatic wrapping
- [ ] Print styled text with style application
- [ ] Print any Renderable with measurement and rendering
- [ ] Style stack: push/pop for nested styling
- [ ] Markup string parsing and rendering
- [ ] Recording mode for test capture

### Color System Requirements
- [ ] Auto-detect color capability from environment
- [ ] NO_COLOR environment variable respected
- [ ] Force color override option
- [ ] Color downgrade for limited terminals

### Output Requirements
- [ ] All output respects current width
- [ ] Long lines wrapped at word boundaries
- [ ] Style reset at end of output
- [ ] No raw escape codes in output (sanitized)

### Logging Requirements
- [ ] tracing::debug! for width detection
- [ ] tracing::debug! for color system detection
- [ ] tracing::trace! for style stack operations
- [ ] tracing::info! for recording start/stop

### Test Coverage
- [ ] Unit tests for width handling
- [ ] Unit tests for style stack
- [ ] Unit tests for color system detection
- [ ] Integration tests with renderables
- [ ] Capture/assertion tests for output verification","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:02:54.445310573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:42:54.623192036Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:42:54.623170485Z","close_reason":"Console abstraction implemented with segment-first design, width-aware wrapping, style stack, capture mode, one-writer safety (16 unit tests + 3 doc tests)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3mo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":311,"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ho.7","title":"Implement logging integration","description":"# Implement logging integration (rich-style formatting on top of tracing)

## Objective
Provide an optional logging layer that formats `tracing` events into styled output (Segments) in a way that is:
- readable for humans
- deterministic for tests
- compatible with ftui’s one-writer routing

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/src/logging.rs`

## Scope
- A formatter/subscriber (or layer) that:
  - applies per-level styling (error/warn/info/debug/trace)
  - supports timestamps (configurable)
  - optionally shows source location (file:line)
  - formats structured fields consistently

## One-Writer Rule (Critical)
- This integration must not write directly to stdout/stderr in a way that can interleave with ftui rendering.
- Output should be routed into a sink that can be:
  - captured for tests, or
  - forwarded via ftui’s `LogSink`/TerminalWriter policy.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for formatting choices (stable snapshots of formatted lines/segments).
- Concurrency test: multiple threads logging cannot interleave within a single formatted record.
- Integration test (optional) that shows logs routed into the harness log region.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A tracing integration exists (subscriber/layer) with configurable formatting.
- [ ] Output is Segment-based and can be captured deterministically in tests.
- [ ] One-writer routing is respected (no hidden direct terminal writes).
- [ ] Unit tests cover formatting and level styling.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:29.952985639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:15:48.513357374Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:15:48.513267144Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","depends_on_id":"bd-10x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3e8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":314,"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}

## E2E Test Scenarios

### Console Pipeline E2E
```rust
#[test]
fn e2e_console_full_render() {
    // Setup
    let mut console = Console::with_width(80);
    console.begin_capture();
    
    // Render complex content
    console.rule(Some("Header"));
    console.print(Panel::new(Text::from("Content")).with_title("Box"));
    console.print(Columns::new(vec![col1, col2]));
    console.rule(None);
    
    // Verify
    let segments = console.end_capture();
    assert!(!segments.is_empty());
    assert!(segments.iter().any(|s| s.text.contains("Header")));
    // ... more assertions
}
```

### Measurement Chain E2E
```rust
#[test]
fn e2e_measurement_chain() {
    let content = Group::new(vec![
        Box::new(Padding::new(
            Panel::new(Text::from("Inner")),
            Sides::all(2)
        )),
        Box::new(Rule::new()),
    ]);
    
    let measurement = content.measure(&console, 80);
    assert!(measurement.minimum <= measurement.maximum);
    assert!(measurement.maximum <= 80);
    
    let rendered = content.render(&console, 80);
    for line in &rendered.lines {
        let width: usize = line.iter().map(|s| s.cell_length()).sum();
        assert!(width <= 80);
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- `tracing::info!` for console output operations
- `tracing::debug!` for measurement calculations
- `tracing::trace!` for segment construction
- Live display refreshes logged at trace level

## Coverage Targets
- Line coverage: >= 90%
- Branch coverage: >= 85%

Dependencies: All Rich Core modules, Testing infrastructure (bd-10i.11)


## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-1i67","title":"Animation Choreographer","description":"## Overview
Provide high-level animation composition system for coordinating complex multi-element animations.

## Rationale
- Individual animations are simple but coordination is hard
- Staggered, parallel, and sequential timing needed
- Animation groups should share lifecycle
- Enables polished onboarding and transitions

## Core Components
1. **Timeline**: Sequence of animation events with timing
2. **Animation Group**: Multiple animations with shared control
3. **Stagger**: Apply delay between group members
4. **Callbacks**: Events at animation milestones
5. **Presets**: Common patterns (fade-in-group, cascade, etc.)

## Implementation Strategy
- Build on existing Animation types in ftui-widgets
- Create Choreographer in ftui-extras
- Integrate with Tick events from ftui-runtime
- Provide builder API for timeline construction

## Usage Example
Choreographer::new()
    .add(fade_in(header), Duration::ZERO)
    .add_staggered(items.map(slide_in), ms(50))
    .on_complete(|| show_content())
    .start()

## Preset Animations
- cascade_in: Staggered entrance from top
- fan_out: Elements spread from center
- typewriter: Character-by-character reveal
- pulse_sequence: Attention-grabbing highlight

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for timeline calculation
- Snapshot tests at key animation frames
- Integration tests for callback timing
- Performance tests for large animation groups

## Success Criteria
- Complex animations can be composed declaratively
- Animations stay synchronized across elements
- Preset library covers common use cases

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-1i67.1","title":"Timeline Data Structure","description":"Sequence of animation events with timing.

## Requirements
- Timeline with keyed events
- Absolute and relative timing
- Loop and repeat support
- Pause/resume/seek control

## Proposed API

```rust
pub struct Timeline<M> {
    events: Vec<TimelineEvent<M>>,
    duration: Duration,
    loop_count: LoopCount,
    state: PlaybackState,
    current_time: Duration,
}

pub struct TimelineEvent<M> {
    pub offset: Duration,       // When event starts
    pub duration: Duration,     // How long it runs
    pub animation: Box<dyn Animation<M>>,
    pub label: Option<String>,  // For seeking by name
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
pub enum LoopCount {
    Once,
    Times(u32),
    Infinite,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
pub enum PlaybackState {
    Idle,
    Playing,
    Paused,
    Finished,
}

impl<M> Timeline<M> {
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    
    /// Add event at absolute offset
    pub fn add(self, offset: Duration, anim: impl Animation<M>) -> Self;
    
    /// Add event relative to last event's end
    pub fn then(self, anim: impl Animation<M>) -> Self;
    
    /// Add event with label
    pub fn add_labeled(self, label: &str, offset: Duration, anim: impl Animation<M>) -> Self;
    
    /// Set loop behavior
    pub fn loop_count(self, count: LoopCount) -> Self;
    
    /// Playback control
    pub fn play(&mut self);
    pub fn pause(&mut self);
    pub fn resume(&mut self);
    pub fn stop(&mut self);
    pub fn seek(&mut self, time: Duration);
    pub fn seek_label(&mut self, label: &str);
    
    /// Process tick, return messages
    pub fn tick(&mut self, delta: Duration) -> Vec<M>;
    
    /// Current progress 0.0 - 1.0
    pub fn progress(&self) -> f32;
    
    /// Check if complete
    pub fn is_finished(&self) -> bool;
}
```

## Implementation Details

### Event Scheduling
Store events sorted by offset. On each tick:
1. Advance current_time by delta
2. Find events that should be active (offset <= current_time < offset + duration)
3. Call tick() on each active animation
4. Collect messages

### Looping
When current_time >= duration and loop_count allows:
- Reset current_time to 0
- Decrement loop counter (if finite)
- Reset all animations

- test_sequential_events: A then B then C
- test_overlapping_events: Parallel animations
- test_loop_finite: Loops exact count

- test_seek_label: Jump to labeled event

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","title":"Design: golden trace format for terminal sessions (native + web)","description":"Goal
- Define a stable, versioned trace format we can use to reproduce bugs and gate regressions across core/web/native/remote.

Format Requirements
- Inputs
  - output byte streams (VT/ANSI) and/or patches
  - input events (keyboard/mouse/touch/IME)
  - resize events
  - tick/time deltas (explicitly injected)
- Checkpoints
  - frame hashes and/or patch hashes
  - optional grid snapshots for debugging
- Metadata
  - run_id, git_sha/build_id, seed
  - size, DPR (web), terminal profile/capabilities, TERM identity (remote)
  - perf counters: timing histograms, patch sizes, bytes in/out

Design Constraints
- Forward-migratable schema (versioned, with strict validation).
- Reducible/minimizable: traces must be amenable to automatic minimization (delta debugging).

Acceptance Criteria
- Trace format is versioned and forward-migratable.
- Can be produced by native E2E scripts, web harnesses, and remote PTY sessions.
- Validator produces actionable errors (line + field context).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.10","title":"Alien Artifact: statistical perf regression detection (e-values/Bayes)","description":"Goal
- Reduce perf-gate false positives while catching real regressions using principled statistics.

Alien Artifact Techniques
- Anytime-valid hypothesis testing via e-values/e-processes (optional stopping safe).
- Bayesian performance model (posterior over regression magnitude) + Bayes factors for evidence strength.
- Explicit expected-loss decision rule:
  - define loss matrix for false positive (wasted engineer time) vs false negative (shipping regression)
  - choose thresholds/policies that minimize expected loss under measured noise.

Deliverables
- A confidence report and evidence ledger per perf gate:
  - p-value alternatives (e-values), posterior summaries, and Bayes factors
  - dataset summary (runs, variance, hardware)
  - decision + rationale
- Clear override/rollback policy when noise dominates.

Acceptance Criteria
- Perf gates become robust: materially fewer flaky failures without letting real regressions through.
- Outputs are machine-readable and human-auditable.

-{"id":"bd-lff4p.5.13","title":"Alien Artifact: failing-trace minimizer (delta debugging + VOI)","description":"Goal
- When a golden trace fails (checksum mismatch), automatically minimize it to the smallest repro trace that still fails.

Why (User Value)
- Makes regressions debuggable in minutes: a minimized trace is actionable and can be attached to issues/PRs.

Techniques (Alien Artifact)
- Start with classic delta debugging (ddmin) on the event stream.
- Upgrade with Value-of-Information (VOI) heuristics to pick the next reduction step that maximizes expected information gain per replay cost.
- Maintain an evidence ledger: each reduction attempt logs (candidate size, replay time, pass/fail, inferred minimality bounds).

Deliverables
- CLI/API to minimize a trace given a predicate ("replay mismatches").
- Produces a new trace file + a minimization report (JSON/JSONL).

Acceptance Criteria
- On a representative failing trace, produces >=10x size reduction while preserving the failure.
- Deterministic: repeated minimization yields identical minimized trace on same build.
- Integrates into CI failure artifacts (minimized trace attached when feasible/time-bounded).

- Finds at least one non-trivial bug during development (expectation: state machines are hard).
